title: best scales for herbalists
intro_desc: Apothecary scales are precision instruments used by apothecaries to measure the weight of medicinal substances with high accuracy. These scales are typically made of brass, copper, or other durable materials and feature a delicate balance system to ensure precise readings. Apothecaries rely on these scales to accurately measure the weight of ingredients in their formulations, ensuring the correct proportions of active and inactive ingredients. This is crucial in creating consistent and effective medicinal preparations. By using apothecary scales, apothecaries can ensure the quality and efficacy of their products, providing reliable treatments for their patients.

products.product_desc: These scales feature an easy to use digital kitchen scale design, making it simple for apothecaries to accurately measure ingredients and substances. Both English and metric scales are provided, giving users the flexibility to choose their preferred units of measurement. The thin and lightweight compact design allows for easy storage and portability, making it an ideal addition to any apothecary's workspace. Additionally, the auto shut-off feature helps conserve battery life, while the tare function enables users to zero out containers and start fresh. The bright and easy to read LED screen supports multiple measurement units, including kg, ml, and oz, and is a reliable and versatile kitchen tool for tracking nutritional values and fiber intake.
products.product_cons_desc: Some users reported issues with the accuracy and reliability of the scales, citing complaints such as the scale being agonizingly slow to measure, displaying inaccurate readings, losing calibration over time, and requiring manual input of macros for food. Additionally, some users experienced problems with the display, including it darkening after inactivity and being difficult to read, as well as the weight changing when nothing is interacting with the scale. Furthermore, issues with the functionality of the scales included the weight not resetting to zero after removing items, significant lag in response time for measurements, and the scale stalling or jumping by tens of grams during measurements.
